output.code:
import re

raw = text

spans = []

# find all occurrences of ﴿...﴾
for m in re.finditer(r'﴿(.*?)﴾', raw, flags=re.S):
    inner = m.group(1)
    # trim edge whitespace and punctuation per rule
    trimmed = inner.strip(" \n\t\"'«»()[]{}<>،؛:.,!؟…ـ*")
    if len(trimmed) < 10:
        continue
    start = raw.find(trimmed, m.start(), m.end())
    if start == -1:
        # try find globally
        start = raw.find(trimmed)
    end = start + len(trimmed)
    spans.append((start,end,trimmed,'Aya', m.start(), m.end()))  # store also orig group bounds

# find all occurrences inside double quotes "..."
for m in re.finditer(r'"(.*?)"', raw, flags=re.S):
    inner = m.group(1)
    trimmed = inner.strip(" \n\t\"'«»()[]{}<>،؛:.,!؟…ـ")
    if len(trimmed) < 10:
        continue
    # find local cues within ±64 chars
    left = max(0, m.start()-64)
    right = min(len(raw), m.end()+64)
    context = raw[left:right]
    # cues
    aya_cues = ['قال الله', 'قال الله تعالى', 'يقول الله', 'قال تعالى', 'القرآن', 'سورة', 'الآية', 'قال الله تعالى','يقول الله تعالى']
    hadeeth_cues = ['قال رسول', 'قال النبي', 'قال رسول الله', 'قال النبي ﷺ','قال رسول الله ﷺ','روي عن','في الحديث','رواه','أخرجه','أخرجه البخاري','أخرجه مسلم','عن النبي']
    # detect
    is_h = any(c in context for c in hadeeth_cues)
    is_a = any(c in context for c in aya_cues)
    typ = 'Hadeeth' if is_h and not is_a else ('Aya' if is_a and not is_h else ( 'Hadeeth' if is_h else 'Aya'))
    # find exact position of trimmed within m bounds
    start = raw.find(trimmed, m.start(), m.end())
    if start == -1:
        start = raw.find(trimmed)
    end = start + len(trimmed)
    spans.append((start,end,trimmed, 'Hadeeth' if typ=='Hadeeth' else 'Aya', m.start(), m.end()))

# remove duplicates and sort by start
# but must include duplicates if different occurrences; here we used positions so include all
spans_sorted = sorted(spans, key=lambda x: x[0])
len(spans_sorted), spans_sorted[:20]

for s in spans_sorted:
    start,end,txt,typ,orig_s,orig_e = s
    context = raw[max(0,start-80):min(len(raw),end+80)]
    print("SPAN:", txt)
    print("start,end", start,end, "detected:", typ)
    print("context:", repr(context))
    print("-"*80)

# Search for any other ﴾ or quotes etc we might have missed
# Find all occurrences of Arabic quotation marks like « » or single quotes? but seems not.
# Print all double-quoted contents we extracted
for s in spans_sorted:
    print(s[2], s[3])

for m in re.finditer(r'بِ\w+بَيْع', raw):
    print("match", m.group(0), m.start(), m.end())

for m in re.finditer('الْفَوْزُ', raw):
    print(m.start(), raw[m.start()-60:m.start()+60])

s = 'بِبَيْعِكُمُ الَّذِي بَايَعْتُمْ بِهِ وَذَلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ'
raw.find(s)

final = []
# use existing spans_sorted but update types for last one index 3961-> Hadeeth
for s in spans_sorted:
    start,end,txt,typ,_,_ = s
    if txt.startswith('ثلاث فيهنّ البركة'):
        typ = 'Hadeeth'
    final.append((start,end,txt, 'Hadeeth' if typ=='Hadeeth' else 'Aya'))

# add the fragment at 2802
frag = 'بِبَيْعِكُمُ الَّذِي بَايَعْتُمْ بِهِ وَذَلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ'
i = raw.find(frag)
i, i+len(frag), raw[i:i+len(frag)] == frag
